description: AJR, American Journal of Roentgenology is an active publication covering original articles on all aspects of general and diagnostic radiology, covering all current modalities, including MRI. It was established in 1906 and is published monthly by American Roentgen Ray Society. The journal welcomes submissions of high-quality research papers, reviews, case studies, and clinical trials related to the field of radiology. It provides a forum for radiologists, medical physicists, and other healthcare professionals to share their findings and contribute to the growth and development of radiology as a discipline. The journal not only aims to advance scientific knowledge but also supports the translation of research into clinical practice, advancing patient care. AJR, American Journal of Roentgenology can be accessed online at: https://www.ajronline.org Publication History: - American Journal of Roentgenology 0092-5381 (until 1923) USA United States - American Journal of Roentgenology and Radium Therapy 0092-5632 (until 1952) USA United States - American Journal of Roentgenology, Radium Therapy and Nuclear Medicine 0002-9580 (until 1976) USA United States - American Quarterly of Roentgenology (until 1913) USA United States
subject: Health & Medical Sciences
title: AJR, American Journal of Roentgenology
